# Attest

> Delegated authorization protocol for AI agent pipelines.

Attest gives AI agents signed, scoped credentials tied to human intent. It lets you issue a root credential when a human approves a task, delegate narrower credentials to sub-agents, enforce tool-call authorization at runtime, and revoke the full delegation tree instantly. Every action produces a cryptographic evidence trail.

## Core concepts

- **Delegated authority**: A human authorizes a task. The orchestrator gets a signed JWT credential scoped to that task. Sub-agents receive narrower child credentials. Authority flows downward, never sideways.
- **Intent binding**: The human's instruction is SHA-256 hashed and embedded in the credential. Any mutation to the original intent is detectable.
- **Scope enforcement**: Each credential carries an explicit scope array (e.g. `["gmail:send", "research:read"]`). Child credentials must be strict subsets of their parent.
- **Revocation**: Revoking a parent credential cascades to all children. Revocation is checked at enforcement time.
- **Evidence and provenance**: Every issuance, delegation, tool call, and revocation is recorded in an append-only audit log with hash-chained entries.

## Links

- Homepage: https://attestdev.com
- API docs: https://attestdev.com/docs.html
- MCP quickstart: https://attestdev.com/mcp.html
- Evidence verifier: https://attestdev.com/verify.html
- Interactive demo: https://attestdev.com/demo.html
- GitHub: https://github.com/chudah1/attest-dev
- npm SDK: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@attest-dev/sdk
- PyPI SDK: https://pypi.org/project/attest-sdk/
- API endpoint: https://api.attestdev.com
- IETF Internet-Draft: draft-yakung-oauth-agent-attestation-00
- Full context for LLMs: https://attestdev.com/llms-full.txt
